proposed.name: dag-field-presence
proposed.claim: Field presence on a DAG node is determined by structural type; compounds and inferences require deps, non-contract directives require deps or a stipulation artifact, declared contracts may be standalone, and contract fields are directive-only

rules.then: id, type, kind, domain, tags, claim, subjects, status, created are all valid fields
artifact (required) and evidence (dated, append-only) are valid; deps, materialized, rules, invariants are not meaningful
The node must have a non-empty deps array - a compound by definition composes multiple deps
The node must have a non-empty deps array - a conclusion traces its grounding even when its claim exceeds it

rules.scenario: Non-contract directive grounds in deps or stipulation
rules.given: A node has type: directive and does not carry rules or invariants
rules.then: The node must have non-empty deps or a stipulation artifact (plan:/user:/session:/document:) - adoption is grounded in beliefs or in the record of the adoption event

rules.given: A node has type: directive and carries non-empty rules or invariants
rules.then: The node may have empty deps - contracts can be declared (formalize policy standalone) or derived; both forms are valid

invariants: Active non-contract directives carry deps or a stipulation artifact
Contract fields (contract, rules, invariants) are directive-only
Fields are emitted in a stable schema-defined order
Fields absent in the source JSON are not emitted on round-trip (presence tracked via _keys)
tags defaults to empty list when absent from source

reasoning: cb-schema-v2: deps requirements re-attach per type (compounds, inferences, non-contract directives under the D4 grounding rule); contract fields are directive-only. The stale 'confidence' shared-field reference is dropped in passing.
